title: poa pratensis var. pubescens
intro: Poa pratensis var. pubescens, also known as the hairy Kentucky bluegrass or hairy meadow grass, is a perennial grass species that belongs to the family Poaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it a potential treatment for various health issues. The plant is said to have health benefits such as reducing fever, soothing digestive problems, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues. Poa pratensis var. pubescens can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for consumption, although further research is necessary to confirm its efficacy and safety.
